sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: small amounts can change a whole meal. that leverage matters when the fat quality is good. can help meals feel more complete and usable. fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. efficient energy source. that can be a strength in the right context.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: small amounts can change a whole meal. that leverage matters when the fat quality is good. can help meals feel more complete and usable. fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. efficient energy source. that can be a strength in the right context.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: not a complete food on its own. even the better fats are still just part of a meal. calorie density is brutally high. tiny serving errors matter here. easy to overpour without noticing. practical portion creep is real.

closing.summary: Ghee does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only in small amounts when the cooking job matters more than the nutrition case.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only in small amounts when the cooking job matters more than the nutrition case.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Vitamins are carrying this food most, while fats are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
